text: 0.9g of carbs. and that is basically the whole nutrition story.

pros first: a useful low-calorie flavor tool. as a swap for heavy sauces or dressings, it has some real value. can still earn points as a better swap. the category should reward harm reduction when it is real. use case can matter more than macros. this category is really about function and trade-offs.

now the downsides: health claims are usually exaggerated. the item tends to be oversold way beyond what it deserves. rarely deserves to be treated as a foundation food. most of these are situational, not core nutrition plays. easy to overrate or misuse. this category attracts strong marketing and habits.
D tier. Best treated as an occasional context item, not a nutritional base.
